revision.text:
{{神社 |画像= |名称=津神社 |所在地=岐阜県岐阜市曽我屋字屋敷1631 |位置= |祭神=縣須美命 [[武渟川別|武渟川別命]] |社格=郷社 |創建=6世紀 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }}
'''津神社'''（つじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[伊自良川]]に架かる[[島大橋有料道路|島大橋]]の西岸にある。
[[美濃国]][[方県郡]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の方県津神社とされている。但し、美濃国方県郡の方県津神社に関しては他にも[[方県津神社]]、懸神社などの幾つかの論社がある。
== 沿革 == 社伝によると、[[推古天皇]]の時代、[[伊勢国]][[安濃郡 (伊勢国)|安濃郡]]津（現・[[三重県]][[津市]]）より勧請されたという（神社名は不明）。別の説では[[加賀国]][[江沼郡]]（現･[[石川県]][[加賀市]]）の潮津神社の勧請という。
[[江戸時代]]は「方県津大明神」と称していたが、明治6年（[[1873年]]）に津神社に改称している。
== 祭神 == *縣須美命 :詳細は不明 *[[武渟川別|武渟川別命]]
== 所在地 == *岐阜県岐阜市曽我屋字屋敷1631
== 交通機関 == *[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]大野真正北方線「曽我屋東」バス停下車 **[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北口）8番のりば、[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）6番のりばより「O80 |[[リバーサイドモール]]（[[大縄場大橋]]経由）」「O85 [[大野バスセンター]]」行き
{{DEFAULTSORT:つしんしや}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜市]]

{{神社 |画像= |名称=津神社 |所在地=岐阜県岐阜市曽我屋字屋敷1631 |位置= |祭神=縣須美命 [[武渟川別|武渟川別命]] |社格=郷社 |創建=6世紀 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== Location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: * From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. * Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).
{{Commonscat|Tsu-jinja}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsu Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[ja:津神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== Location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: * From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. * Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."

However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.

The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=draft}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|12=draft}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市|12=draft}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市|12=draft}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=draft}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=draft}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車|12=draft}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅|12=draft}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|12=draft}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|Tsu Shrine]] | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Tsu Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋|12=draft}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|ja|伊自良川|12=draft}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=draft}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=draft}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|12=draft}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市|12=draft}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市|12=draft}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=draft}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=draft}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車|12=draft}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅|12=draft}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|12=draft}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2022年11月}} {{神社 |画像=[[File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg|250px|津神社]] |名称=津神社 |所在地=岐阜県岐阜市曽我屋字屋敷1631 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|42.48|N|136|43|15.68|E|scale:30000}} |祭神=縣須美命 [[武渟川別|武渟川別命]] |社格=郷社 |創建=6世紀 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }} {{Commonscat|Tsu-jinja}} '''津神社'''（つじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[伊自良川]]に架かる[[島大橋]]の西岸にある。
[[美濃国]][[方県郡]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の方県津神社とされている。但し、美濃国方県郡の方県津神社に関しては他にも[[方県津神社]]、[[縣神社 (岐阜市)|縣神社]]などの幾つかの論社がある。
== 沿革 == 社伝によると、[[推古天皇]]の時代、[[伊勢国]][[安濃郡 (三重県)|安濃郡]]津（現・[[三重県]][[津市]]）より[[勧請]]されたという。勧請元の神社名は不明である。
別の説では[[加賀国]][[江沼郡]]（現･[[石川県]][[加賀市]]）の潮津神社の勧請という。
[[江戸時代]]は「方県津大明神」と称していたが、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に津神社に改称している。
== 祭神 == *縣須美命 :詳細は不明 *[[武渟川別|武渟川別命]]
== 所在地 == *岐阜県岐阜市曽我屋字屋敷1631
== 交通機関 == *[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]大野真正北方線「曽我屋東」バス停下車 **[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北口）8番のりば、[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）6番のりばより「O80 [[リバーサイドモール]]（[[大縄場大橋]]経由）」「O85 [[大野バスセンター]]」行き *岐阜バス曽我屋線「曽我屋口」バス停下車 **JR岐阜駅バスターミナル（岐阜駅北口）8番のりば、[[岐阜バスターミナル]]（名鉄岐阜駅南）Dのりばより「K25 東改田」「K26 東改田（寺田ガーデン経由）」行き
{{DEFAULTSORT:つしんしや}} [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]]

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Bridge|qq=|1=Shima Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476272}} over the [[:en:Ijira River|Ijira River]].

The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=|1=Agata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608136}}.

== History ==
According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano District|qq=|1=Ano District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946985}}, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.

An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], Ishikawa Prefecture).

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (details unknown)
* {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}

== location ==
* Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan

== Access ==
* By [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]]:
** From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop.
** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Bridge|qq=|1=Shima Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476272}} over the [[:en:Ijira River|Ijira River]].

The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=|1=Agata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608136}}.

== History ==
According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano District|qq=|1=Ano District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946985}}, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.

An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], Ishikawa Prefecture).

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (details unknown)
* {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}

== location ==
* Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan

== Access ==
* By [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]]:
** From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop.
** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608136}}.

== History ==
According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano District|1=Ano District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946985}}, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.

An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], Ishikawa Prefecture).

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Bridge|1=Shima Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476272}} over the [[:en:Ijira River|Ijira River]].

The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608136}}.

== History ==
According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano District|1=Ano District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946985}}, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.

An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], Ishikawa Prefecture).

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (details unknown)
* {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}

** From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop.
** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Bridge|1=Shima Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476272}} over the [[:en:Ijira River|Ijira River]].

The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608136}}.

== History ==
According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano District|1=Ano District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946985}}, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.

An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], Ishikawa Prefecture).

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (details unknown)
* {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}

== location ==
* Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan

** From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop.
** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

** From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop.
** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Bridge|1=Shima Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476272}} over the [[:en:Ijira River|Ijira River]].

The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608136}}.

== History ==
According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano District|1=Ano District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946985}}, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.

An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], Ishikawa Prefecture).

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (details unknown)
* {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}

== location ==
* Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan

== Access ==
* By [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]]:
** From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop.
** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsu-jinja]][[ja:津神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsu-jinja]][[ja:津神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsu-jinja]][[ja:津神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11556358]] Wikidata name: Tsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.428467|N|136.721022|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Gifu|WD=Q45798}} }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Bridge|1=Shima Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476272}} over the [[:en:Ijira River|Ijira River]].
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608136}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano District|1=Ano District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946985}}, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]]: ** From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).
{{Commonscat|Tsu-jinja}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|津神社|version=92662429|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:津神社]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsu-jinja]][[ja:津神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsu-jinja]][[ja:津神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsu-jinja]][[ja:津神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11556358]] Wikidata name: Tsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.428467|N|136.721022|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Gifu|WD=Q45798}} }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu City}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Bridge|1=Shima Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476272}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|en|Ijira River|WD=Q332939|lt=Ijira River}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608136}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano District|1=Ano District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946985}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|Kaga, Ishikawa|WD=Q120103|lt=Kaga, Ishikawa}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|en|Gifu Station|WD=Q1144885|lt=Gifu Station}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|en|Meitetsu Gifu Station|WD=Q1109902|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).
{{Commonscat|Tsu-jinja}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|津神社|version=92662429|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:津神社]]
