revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsu-jinja]][[ja:津神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsu-jinja]][[ja:津神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsu-jinja]][[ja:津神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11556358]] Wikidata name: Tsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsu jinja2009-3.jpg | name = Tsu Shrine (津神社) | location = Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.428467|N|136.721022|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = 6th century | architecture = | festivals = | rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Gifu|WD=Q45798}} }}
'''Tsu Shrine''' (津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu City}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is situated west of the {{ill|Shima Bridge|ja|島大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Bridge|1=Shima Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476272}} over the {{ill|Ijira River|en|Ijira River|WD=Q332939|lt=Ijira River}}.
The shrine is believed to correspond to the {{ill|Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}-listed shrine of the Hōgō District in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608136}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano District|1=Ano District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946985}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|Kaga, Ishikawa|WD=Q120103|lt=Kaga, Ishikawa}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|en|Gifu Station|WD=Q1144885|lt=Gifu Station}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|en|Meitetsu Gifu Station|WD=Q1109902|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).

During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."

However, there are competing theories about the true identity of the Hōgō-gun Tsu Shrine, with other candidates including {{ill|Hōgō Tsu Shrine|ja|方県津神社|1=Katagatatsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgō Tsu Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagatatsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608136}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, Tsu Shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} with a deity transferred from Tsu in {{ill|Ano District|ja|安濃郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano District|1=Q10946985|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946985}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu, Mie|1=Tsu, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture). The original shrine from which it was transferred is unknown.
An alternative theory suggests the shrine's origin lies in a transfer from {{ill|Shiozu Shrine|ja|潮津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|Kaga, Ishikawa|WD=Q120103|lt=Kaga, Ishikawa|1=Kaga, Ishikawa}}, Ishikawa Prefecture).
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as "Hōgō Tsu Daimyōjin." In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Tsu Shrine."
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja|縣須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Sumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (details unknown) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}
== location == * Sogaya, Yashiki 1631, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * By {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}: ** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|en|Gifu Station|WD=Q1144885|lt=Gifu Station|1=Gifu Station}} (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|en|Meitetsu Gifu Station|WD=Q1109902|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station|1=Meitetsu Gifu Station}} (west, platform 6), take "O80 Riverside Mall (via Ōnawa Bridge)" or "O85 Ōno Bus Center" to "Sogaya Higashi" stop. ** Alternatively, take the Sogaya Line ("K25 Higashikaiden" or "K26 Higashikaiden via Terada Garden") to "Sogaya-guchi" stop from JR Gifu Station (north exit, bus terminal platform 8) or Gifu Bus Terminal (south of Meitetsu Gifu Station, platform D).
